title: MiFID II Clock Synchronization and Timestamp Quality for Reporting

compliance.assurance_rationale: MiFID II Art.25 — UC provides continuous Splunk telemetry evidence for this regulatory requirement, uplifted from contributing to partial after review confirmed the SPL captures relevant monitoring signals.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that MiFID II Art.25 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.5.7: MiFID II Clock Synchronization and Timestamp Quality for Reporting.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.5.7' running on sourcetype Unix:Version and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=os` `sourcetype="Unix:Version"` OR `sourcetype="chrony:tracking"` (host, SystemTime, LeapStatus, LastOffset, RMSOffset); `index=trading` `sourcetype="_json"` `source="http:order_lifecycle"` (host, reported_event_epoch_ms, _time)
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder (core platform), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833)
spl:
index=trading sourcetype="_json" source="http:order_lifecycle" earliest=-4h isnotnull(reported_event_epoch_ms)
| eval skew_ms=abs((reported_event_epoch_ms/1000)-_time)*1000
| stats median(skew_ms) as p50_skew, perc95(skew_ms) as p95_skew, max(skew_ms) as max_skew by host
| where p95_skew>250 OR max_skew>1000
| sort - p95_skew
description: RTS 25 mandates traceable UTC synchronization for algorithmic and high-frequency activity reporting. Comparing application-reported event time to Splunk ingestion `_time` highlights skewed hosts or malformed timestamps that could invalidate best execution and transaction reports.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for MiFID II compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes os, trading and sourcetypes Unix:Version, _json, chrony:tracking. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Forward `chrony` or `ntpq` telemetry from trading servers; (2) align JSON `reported_event_epoch_ms` with exchange event time definitions; (3) alert on hosts breaching your documented max skew (e.g. 250 ms); (4) exclude batch backfills with a `ingest_mode` flag; (5) document remediation in runbooks tied to RTS 25 testing. Indexes required: `index=os`, `index=trading`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=Unix:Version`, `sourcetype=_json`, `sourcetype=chrony:tracking`.
visualization: Table (host skew stats), Timechart (median skew by host), Single value (hosts breaching threshold).

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux

knownFalsePositives: Reporting gaps during venue maintenance, scheduled feed restatements, or vendor data corrections.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Universal Forwarder (core platform), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=os` `sourcetype="Unix:Version"` OR `sourcetype="chrony:tracking"` (host, SystemTime, LeapStatus, LastOffset, RMSOffset); `index=trading` `sourcetype="_json"` `source="http:order_lifecycle"` (host, reported_event_epoch_ms, _time).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Forward `chrony` or `ntpq` telemetry from trading servers; (2) align JSON `reported_event_epoch_ms` with exchange event time definitions; (3) alert on hosts breaching your documented max skew (e.g. 250 ms); (4) exclude batch backfills with a `ingest_mode` flag; (5) document remediation in runbooks tied to RTS 25 testing.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=trading sourcetype="_json" source="http:order_lifecycle" earliest=-4h isnotnull(reported_event_epoch_ms)
| eval skew_ms=abs((reported_event_epoch_ms/1000)-_time)*1000
| stats median(skew_ms) as p50_skew, perc95(skew_ms) as p95_skew, max(skew_ms) as max_skew by host
| where p95_skew>250 OR max_skew>1000
| sort - p95_skew
```

Understanding this SPL

**MiFID II Clock Synchronization and Timestamp Quality for Reporting (RTS 25)** — RTS 25 mandates traceable UTC synchronization for algorithmic and high-frequency activity reporting. Comparing application-reported event time to Splunk ingestion `_time` highlights skewed hosts or malformed timestamps that could invalidate best execution and transaction reports.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=os` `sourcetype="Unix:Version"` OR `sourcetype="chrony:tracking"` (host, SystemTime, LeapStatus, LastOffset, RMSOffset); `index=trading` `sourcetype="_json"` `source="http:order_lifecycle"` (host, reported_event_epoch_ms, _time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Universal Forwarder (core platform), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: trading; **sourcetype**: _json. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=trading, sourcetype="_json", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **skew_ms** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where p95_skew>250 OR max_skew>1000` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (host skew stats), Timechart (median skew by host), Single value (hosts breaching threshold).
grandmaExplanation: We rTS 25 mandates traceable UTC synchronization for algorithmic and high-frequency activity reporting.
